reason: The memory was written, but embedding/index incompatibility prevents retrieval so dispatch sees zero relevant hits.
Relevant memories exist but an incorrect project-scope predicate filters them out during recall.
A recall-time index race hides existing memories and returns no results for the dispatch query.
The recall filter chain removes valid rows before ranking, causing a miss despite stored memory.
Time-window logic excludes relevant persisted memories, so autoRecallForDispatch returns zero hits.
The fallback query path ignores the tier containing the relevant memory and therefore misses it.
Input sanitization alters the query such that existing relevant memories are no longer matched.
Dedup logic collapses valid candidates before final selection, resulting in an empty recall set.
Replication lag means recall reads stale state and misses newly written relevant memory.
Candidate truncation leaves too few rows for thresholding, so no recalled memory is returned.
Identifier drift between write and recall lookups prevents retrieval of memories that do exist.
An outdated recall allowlist filters out the relevant persisted observation types.
Routing to an empty index namespace causes no-hit recall even though memory is present elsewhere.
Timestamp handling falsely marks valid rows stale, so recall omits relevant stored memories.
After denylist filtering, recall fails to backfill with lower-ranked valid hits and returns empty.
Relevant memory is recalled but truncated during context assembly so it is not actually used.
The system recalls memory but puts it in a disabled prompt section that the agent never receives.
Retrieved memory is present but deprioritized to effectively unusable position in final context.
Recalled content is stripped of meaningful rationale before dispatch, so guidance is disregarded.
Memory is recalled but transformed into generic text that no longer influences downstream decisions.
The recall result is fetched but key fields are dropped, so the memory signal is not respected.
Token-pruning keeps lower-value text while cutting recalled memory, which nullifies retrieved context.
Recall appears in preview but is absent in actual worker prompt, meaning fetched memory is ignored.
Hydration returns relevant memory but downstream dedup removes the actionable item before use.
The recalled block is dropped during adapter conversion, so retrieved memory never reaches inference.
Conflict resolution overrides recalled guidance with defaults, so the memory is effectively disregarded.
Transport metadata shows recall happened, but payload narrowing drops the recalled body before execution.
Recalled context is available but planner logic skips it in auto mode, so it is not applied.
Sanitization leaves recalled memory too redacted to be actionable, so the model ignores it.
Prompt delimiters place recalled memory in a segment excluded from model-consumed input.
The finding should be persisted as memory but write gating prevents the memory from being written.
Write failure inside transaction leaves no recorded memory, so prevention data is never registered.
Validation logic aborts persistence, meaning relevant memory is never written in the first place.
A write-path session gate blocks memory creation for auto workflows, causing non-registration.
Queue drops occur before persistence so expected memories are never stored.
Promotion filter parsing excludes valid sessions, preventing memory registration for those findings.
Pre-write dedup rejects a new finding and therefore the corresponding memory is never written.
The write function reports success while skipping insertion, resulting in missing memory registration.
Routing writes to a dead partition drops records so the intended memory never gets stored.
A disabled feature flag bypasses the memory-write branch, leaving findings unregistered.
Enum mapping failure causes the adapter to drop findings instead of persisting them as memory.
Non-network write errors are not retried, so failed memory writes become permanent registration gaps.
Role-based write suppression prevents reviewer findings from being written to long-term memory.
Transient parse failures discard ingest rows instead of retrying, so memory registration is missed.
Required provenance omissions block inserts on one path, so those memories are never written.
